critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — APPROVED-APPLICANT TRAINING BIAS: Training on approved applicants limits the model's validity to that conditional population. Any deployment beyond approved applicants would invalidate the performance claim.

C2 — ALTERNATIVE DATA FCRA COMPLIANCE: Non-traditional data requires careful adverse action disclosures.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — The deployment constraint addresses this precisely: the model is deployed only to applicants who have already cleared human underwriting. This is the same population the model was trained on. The constraint operationalizes the bias mitigation. The performance claim (AUC 0.791) holds for this constrained deployment.

C2 — Compliance concern, not a model flaw. Addressable through process.
adjudication_raw: Debate resolves in one round. Survivorship bias concern is resolved by the documented second-stage deployment constraint. Alternative data is a compliance process matter. Defense wins both points.
